organic.title: Vitamin D deficiency
organic.snippet: Not getting enough vitamin D, also called a vitamin D deficiency, can cause your bones to become thin, brittle, bent or bowed during growth.

organic.title: Vitamin D Deficiency: Symptoms, Treatments, and Causes
organic.snippet: Symptoms of vitamin D deficiency may include fatigue, frequent illness, anxiety, bone pain, and slower wound healing, among others.

peopleAlsoAsk.question: What happens when your vitamin D goes low?
peopleAlsoAsk.title: Vitamin D Deficiency - MedlinePlus
peopleAlsoAsk.snippet: What problems does vitamin D deficiency cause? Vitamin D deficiency can lead to a loss of bone density, which can contribute to osteoporosis and fractures (broken bones). Severe vitamin D deficiency can also lead to other diseases: In children, it can cause rickets.Apr 22, 2024

peopleAlsoAsk.question: What are the 10 signs of low vitamin D?
peopleAlsoAsk.title: 10 Unexpected Signs of a Vitamin D Deficiency
peopleAlsoAsk.snippet:
10 Unexpected Signs of a Vitamin D Deficiency
___Fatigue that won't go away. ...
___Muscle aches and weakness. ...
___Frequent illnesses. ...
___Mood swings or depression. ...
___Persistent back or bone pain. ...
___Unexplained weight gain. ...
___Hair loss. ...
___Slow wound healing.

peopleAlsoAsk.question: How to quickly raise vitamin D levels?
peopleAlsoAsk.title: What Causes Vitamin D Deficiency and How to Treat It - Scripps Health
peopleAlsoAsk.snippet: Increase safe sun exposure: Spending more time in sunlight helps your body produce vitamin D naturally. Your doctor can recommend what is appropriate for your skin type and health. Eat more vitamin D-rich foods: Add fatty fish, egg yolks, fortified dairy and cereals and sun-exposed mushrooms to your diet.Aug 12, 2025

peopleAlsoAsk.question: Which food is rich in vitamin D?
peopleAlsoAsk.title: Vitamin D - The Nutrition Source
peopleAlsoAsk.snippet:
Many foods and supplements are fortified with vitamin D like dairy products and cereals.
___Cod liver oil.
___Salmon.
___Swordfish.
___Tuna fish.
___Orange juice fortified with vitamin D.
___Dairy and plant milks fortified with vitamin D.
___Sardines.
___Beef liver.
